Reserve
An Editorial Sanctuary · Est. 2019

Where lavender
meets stillness.

A private boutique villa quietly nestled between fields of bloom and centuries of stone. Seven suites. One unhurried season. By invitation, and by Gardenias.

Reserve a Stay Discover the House
A Quiet Welcome

An unhurried portrait
of slow living.

The Lavender House is not a hotel. It is a private residence opened to a few, tucked into a sloping field of bloom. Mornings begin with linen drapes catching the first light; evenings end with candle dinners beneath the cypress trees. Every detail is composed — and quietly left alone.

07
Suites
12
Acres
1:1
Service
The Suites

Seven rooms, each composed
like a private letter.

Hand-stitched linens, antique brass, and windows framed for stillness. No two suites are alike — each is its own quiet chapter.

The Rituals

Days arranged like poems.

A handful of unhurried moments, kept simple, kept slow.

01

Lavender Hour

Sunset gathering with chilled rosé and fresh blooms cut by hand each morning.

02

The Long Table

A six-course farm dinner under lanterns. Set once weekly, for house guests only.

03

Quiet Spa

Lavender oil rituals in a candlelit stone bath. By private appointment.

04

Field Walks

A guided amble through the lavender rows at first light, with warm bread to follow.

The House, in Frames

A visual diary.

From the Journal

"To stay here is to remember
the colour of quiet."

Featured in Condé Nast Traveller, AD India, and Kinfolk Vol. 47. The Lavender House is among a quiet new generation of editorial sanctuaries — shaped not for the many, but for the few who notice the details.

Condé Nast· AD· Kinfolk· Vogue Living
"

The most beautifully restrained stay I've had in years. Every corner whispers, never shouts.

Anaïs Laurent
Editor, Maison & Travel
